A. The Contractor shall provide all software licenses, interfaces, services, training, and
support necessary to connect the Hemochron Signature Elites analyzers to the existing
RALS system and ensure compliant data management.

B. Brand Name or Equal: When the government lists a specific brand‑name product in a
solicitation, vendors are not required to offer that exact brand. They may offer a different
product as long as it meets all the important features and performance requirements
listed in the solicitation. These required features are called salient characteristics.

• Clearly identify what product they are offering
• Show how it meets the required characteristics
• Provide supporting information (like brochures, spec sheets, or drawings)
• Describe any changes they would make to meet the requirements

The government will evaluate “equal” products based only on the information the vendor
provides or references. If a vendor doesn’t say they are offering an “equal” product, the
government will assume they are offering the exact brand-name item.

(3) Provide service, support, updates, and hardware maintenance for the technology
license during the contract period.

(4) Provide free upgrades as they become commercially available and at the same time
as commercial customers, including system upgrades that enhance the interface and
support data integrity.

(1) Ensure the RALS software is compatible with Hemochron Signature Elites analyzers
and the VA LIS and electronic patient record system (VistA/CPRS).

(4) Provide and install any third-party software required to support the system
specifications.

(1) Furnish all supplies, equipment, facilities, and services required for delivery,
verification of operation, and installation of the software needed for connectivity
between the equipment and the VA’s LIS.
